channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ganduje Comments on Kwankwaso’s Frequent Party Switches in Nigerian Politics
item.description: Abdullahi Umar Ganduje, the former National Chairman of the All Progressives Congress (APC), recently described Rabiu Musa Kwankwaso, the 2023 presidential candidate of the New Nigeria People’s Party ...

item.title: RBA Governor Warns of Slower Growth and Higher Inflation Amid Energy Price Pressures
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) has raised the official cash rate by 0.25 percentage points to 4.35%, marking the third increase this year and fully reversing the 2025 cuts. Governor Michele Bullo...

item.title: Philip Morris warns Senate in closed hearing about impact of illegal tobacco on legal market
item.description: Philip Morris International provided evidence in a secret Senate hearing in Canberra, warning that illegal tobacco could eliminate the legal cigarette market in Australia by 2030. The company requeste...

item.title: Arunachal Chief Minister Pema Khandu faces public demand to step aside during corruption probe
item.description: In Arunachal Pradesh, several protesters have come forward demanding that the Chief Minister, Pema Khandu, step aside while a formal corruption investigation is underway. The demonstrators voiced thei...

item.title: The Historical and Political Influence of Women Celebrated on Mother’s Day
item.description: Mother’s Day in the United States is often associated with gift-buying and dining out, generating roughly $34 billion in consumer spending. However, the origins of the holiday emphasize far more than ...

item.title: Social media personality arrested for alleged extortion attempt on lawmaker
item.description: Authorities arrested a well-known social media personality for allegedly attempting to extort a lawmaker. The case is currently being investigated by the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI), with l...

item.title: Lubaina Himid’s Venice Biennale Installation Explores Belonging and Discomfort in Britain
item.description: Lubaina Himid’s British pavilion at the 2026 Venice Biennale presents a contemplative exploration of belonging and alienation within contemporary Britain. Through a series of monumental paintings and ...

item.title: National authorities issue warnings as heavy rains and flooding expected across South Africa
item.description: The National Disaster Management Centre (NDMC) has placed teams on standby as South Africa faces heavy rainfall and thunderstorms this week, with totals expected to surpass 80mm in certain areas. Low-...

item.title: Senate Limits Eligibility for Principal Positions to Returning 10th Assembly Senators
item.description: The Nigerian Senate don make one big change for how dem dey select Senate President and other top positions. After one long closed-door meeting on Tuesday, lawmakers agree say only senators wey don se...

item.title: Curtis MacNguyen: From Hedge Fund Mogul to Maggie Q’s Husband
item.description: Curtis MacNguyen, born in 1968 in Vietnam, is a retired hedge fund manager and entrepreneur who married Hollywood actress Maggie Q after her previous engagement to Dylan McDermott ended. Curtis’s earl...

item.title: CPS orders faster charging decisions to address rise in antisemitic hate crimes
item.description: The head of the Crown Prosecution Service (CPS), Stephen Parkinson, has instructed prosecutors in England and Wales to accelerate the handling of hate crime cases following a sharp rise in antisemitic...

item.title: Walking 1,600km Across South Africa to Highlight the Housing Crisis
item.description: Wandile Mthiyane is undertaking a 1,600km walk from Durban to Cape Town, not for adventure or prestige, but to spotlight the deep-rooted housing crisis in South Africa. Despite being offered places in...

item.title: UK Economic Vulnerabilities Amid Gulf Tensions and Rising Debt
item.description: The UK is facing significant economic risks due to rising global tensions in the Gulf and domestic fiscal challenges. The government, led by Prime Minister Keir Starmer and Chancellor Rachel Reeves, h...

item.title: Families of Missing Indigenous Women Continue to Seek Justice Beyond Red Dress Day
item.description: The CBC News video highlights the ongoing pain and advocacy surrounding missing and murdered Indigenous women, girls, and 2SLGBTQI+ people in Canada, through the lens of Red Dress Day. Observed annual...

item.title: CSOs support military’s reintegration programme for repentant Boko Haram fighters amid public concerns
item.description: Civil Society Organisations (CSOs) for Naija don show support for the military's Operation Safe Corridor wey dey try bring repentant Boko Haram and ISWAP fighters back into society. This programme, we...

item.title: Markets Resume Risk-On Mode as U.S. Stocks Rebound Amid Easing Geopolitical Fears
item.description: Wall Street returned to a more risk‑on posture on May 5, 2026, as major U.S. indexes climbed after a string of volatile days driven by Middle East tensions and oil‑price swings. The Dow Jones Industri...

item.title: Kaitlan Collins’ Career Progress, Salary and Net Worth Overview
item.description: Kaitlan Collins, the American journalist born in Prattville, Alabama, has steadily built an impressive career in political journalism, currently estimated to have a net worth of $6 million. Collins jo...

item.title: Gladys Wanga Leads Polls Ahead of 2027 Homa Bay Gubernatorial Election
item.description: Nairobi – The latest opinion poll by Swiss Poll International indicates that incumbent Homa Bay Governor Gladys Wanga remains the most popular candidate ahead of the 2027 General Election. According t...

item.title: ERC allows flexible installment payments and pauses electricity disconnections from May to July 2026
item.description: The Energy Regulatory Commission (ERC) has announced that residential and non-residential consumers who are unable to pay their electricity bills will not face disconnection from May to July 2026. The...

item.title: Man alleges unprovoked police assault at Sydney protest, plans legal action
item.description: Derek Jones, 56, known as “white shirt guy,” claims he was assaulted by police during a protest in Sydney against Israeli President Isaac Herzog in February. The incident, which left Jones with two br...
